title: Truist Financial (TFC) Intrinsic Value — Gemini DCF Analysis
description: Intrinsic value analysis for Truist Financial, noting the limitations of standard free cash flow models for regional banking institutions.
valuation: Standard DCF valuation based on Free Cash Flow is unworkable for a bank. Truist's valuation is more appropriately measured via price-to-tangible-book-value (P/TBV) or dividend discount models, given its structure as a levered balance sheet entity.
methodology: DCF analysis is not applicable to banks. FCF is not a meaningful metric.
